notes: Pope of the early 5th century, known for correspondence with John Chrysostom.

docs.title: Letter CXXX
docs.author: Jerome
docs.excerpt: … to hold fast the faith of the saintly Innocent, the spiritual son of Anastasius and his successor in the apostolic see; and not to receive any foreign doctrine, however wise and discerning you may take yourself to be.

docs.title: Letter 18
docs.author: John Chrysostom
docs.subject_summary: John Chrysostom to Pope Innocent I - first letter from exile
docs.excerpt: … and comforted by the constant and abiding nature of your disposition and confidence, and by revelling in your abundant and genuine love. This is our wall of defense, this is our security, this our calm haven, this our treasure of infinite blessings, this our gladness, and ground of much joy.

docs.title: Letter 130
docs.author: Jerome
